sanctionsEntries.description: OFAC issued new sanctions on 30+ individuals, companies, and vessels connected to Iranian oil following US-Israeli military strikes on Iran (Feb 28). Coordinated with UK OFSI and EU. MAS FATF Statement (Feb 2026 Plenary) requires all Singapore FIs to apply enhanced due diligence to Iran under FSM (Sanctions - Iran) Regulations 2023 - at maximum operational urgency. All 12 International Group P&I clubs confirmed Gulf war risk cancellation effective March 5 - complete commercial insurance blackout in the Persian Gulf. Trump administration announced sovereign war risk guarantee for neutral-flagged tankers under USN escort. General License 8J wind-down provisions remain in effect pending review. War expanded to Indian Ocean (Mar 6): US submarine sank Iranian frigate Iris Dena off Sri Lanka. 329 commercial tankers trapped in the Gulf. Iraq oil output down ~60% (Bloomberg, Mar 8); Kuwait confirmed full output suspension (Mar 10); GCC supply deficit 5.2M bpd - rerouting of crude flows creates new sanctions evasion risk vectors through non-Gulf intermediaries. IRGC seized Liberian-flagged VLCC Pacific Gallant in Gulf of Oman (Mar 10); 28 crew held - shadow fleet and opportunistic seizures expose owners to OFAC secondary sanctions with no P&I coverage. ASEAN-nexus entities with Iranian petrochemical or shadow fleet exposure under elevated screening.
affectedEntities: All ASEAN correspondent banks with Iranian energy exposure
Shadow fleet vessels transiting Strait of Malacca and Sunda Strait
All 12 P&I clubs withdrawing Gulf war risk cover (effective midnight March 5)

sanctionsEntries.description: UN Panel of Experts report identifies 12 ASEAN-registered vessels suspected of ship-to-ship (STS) transfers in Sulu Sea and Celebes Sea. Vessels linked to Iranian crude oil transshipment through Philippine and Indonesian territorial waters. Separately, 500+ STS transfers involving sanctioned Iranian oil documented off Eastern Johor, Malaysia (UANI satellite tracking, 2025 YTD).
affectedEntities: Karimun port (Indonesia) - 18,242 bpd Iranian crude transshipment

sanctionsEntries.description: OFAC designated 29 shadow fleet vessels and associated management firms for transporting Iranian petroleum. Designated entities include UAE-based Phoenix Ship Management FZE. Named vessels include Palau-flagged NEBULA DRIFT and AETHER SAIL, and Panama-flagged TIDAL RHYTHM and VOYAGER HAVEN.

STRASSELINK PTE. LTD. (Singapore) and managing director FADZLON BIN AHMAD designated for providing pilotage services to NITC tankers on 13 occasions (Jan - Aug 2025) as they transited the Strait of Malacca. Vessels discharged Iranian crude via ship-to-ship transfers in Singapore's Eastern Outer Port Limits (EOPL) - enabling over 20 million barrels of Iranian crude oil transfer.

FinCEN Financial Trend Analysis identified approximately $9 billion in potential Iranian shadow banking activity through US correspondent accounts in 2024. Singapore explicitly named as a hub node alongside UAE and Hong Kong. Oil-linked companies accounted for ~$4 billion (44%) of total activity. Iran's CIMS cross-border messaging system used to bypass SWIFT; any ASEAN bank touching CIMS-connected rails faces heightened secondary exposure.
affectedEntities: ASEAN banks with CIMS-linked correspondent exposure

sanctionsEntries.description: UN snapback sanctions formally reimposed on Iran effective September 28, 2025, restoring all measures under UNSC Resolutions 1696, 1737, 1747, 1803, 1835, and 1929. Re-imposes nuclear-related restrictions, arms embargo, travel bans, and asset freezes on designated individuals and entities. Accompanies broader maximum pressure campaign: OFAC designated 875+ persons, vessels, and aircraft under Iran sanctions in 2025 alone.
affectedEntities: ASEAN sovereign wealth funds with Iranian asset exposure

sanctionsEntries.description: Indonesia-based ORIENTAL designated for knowingly exporting and selling over $3.4 million worth of Iranian-origin petrochemical products including polyethylene. Part of a broader US action targeting facilitators of Iran's petroleum and petrochemical trade across multiple jurisdictions.
affectedEntities: ASEAN correspondent banks under secondary sanctions review

redFlagClients.name: STRASSELINK PTE. LTD. (Singapore)
redFlagClients.reason: Directly designated by US State Dept (s2) for enabling 20M barrels of Iranian crude transit via Singapore EOPL. All transactions blocked; immediate account review and SAR filing required. Correspondent banks servicing this entity face secondary sanctions exposure under OFAC Nov 2025 action.

redFlagClients.name: Phoenix Ship Management FZE
redFlagClients.reason: Directly named in OFAC SDN designation (s1) as manager of Iranian petroleum shadow fleet vessels. UAE-based. All transactions blocked; prior OFAC specific license required. Immediate account review and suspicious activity report filing required.

redFlagClients.name: Unnamed ASEAN Correspondent (Iranian Petrochemical Exposure)
redFlagClients.reason: Secondary sanctions risk from FinCEN Oct 2025 report ($9B shadow banking, s4), State Dept Jul 2025 action (s3), and post-strike Feb 2026 designations (s0). Correspondent banking relationships involving Singapore or Malaysian commodity traders with Iranian petrochemical exposure require immediate EDD review. CIMS-linked exposure is a specific red flag.
